_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-12 SPRN Transport Supplies (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-12: SPRN Transport Supplies — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define required supply lists for SPRN transport vehicles, including immediately available supplies and accompanying vehicle supplies, and specify PPE requirements for SPRN transport personnel.

sections.title: Supply Categories
content: SPRN transport supplies are organized into two categories:
1. IMMEDIATELY AVAILABLE SUPPLIES: must be on the primary transport vehicle at time of dispatch
2. ACCOMPANYING VEHICLE SUPPLIES: carried by chase team or second vehicle that travels with the SPRN transport
All SPRN vehicles must undergo pre-transport supply check before accepting a SPRN patient

sections.title: Immediately Available Supplies — Primary Transport Vehicle
content: PPE for all crew members (per Protocol 10-12 PPE section):
— PAPR units (powered air-purifying respirators) with hoods — one per crew member + spares
— Full-face respirator (backup if PAPR unavailable)
— Fluid-resistant coveralls (Tyvek or equivalent) — per crew member + spares
— Double gloves (nitrile) — multiple pairs per crew member
— Boot covers / shoe covers — per crew member
— Head covers — per crew member
— Eye protection (if full-face respirator used instead of PAPR)
Patient care supplies:
— Biohazard bags (red, labeled) — minimum 6
— Bleach solution (1:10 dilution) or EPA-registered HCID disinfectant wipes
— Disposable absorbent pads / chux pads
— Disposable patient care supplies (BP cuff, SpO2 probe, stethoscope — single-use or dedicated SPRN equipment)
— Surgical masks for patient
— Tape and labels for biohazard bagging of patient belongings
— Pre-printed patient transfer documentation forms
Vehicle supplies:
— Biohazard waste container with lid
— Spare PAPR batteries and filters

sections.title: Accompanying Vehicle Supplies — Chase Team Vehicle
content: Additional PPE (backup for primary vehicle crew):
— Spare PAPR units and hoods
— Additional fluid-resistant coveralls
— Additional gloves, boot covers, head covers
Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD) components (if not on primary vehicle):
— MITD/Biocell containment device
— MITD blower motor and power supply
— MITD access ports and cable sets
Additional patient care and decontamination supplies:
— Spare biohazard bags
— Additional disinfectant solution/wipes
— Spare disposable medical supplies
— Patient transfer gurney cover/barrier
Team support:
— Communications equipment (radio, phone)
— Route maps and GPS for MDHHS-coordinated travel plan (Protocol 10-13)
— Crew hydration supplies
— Emergency doffing PPE area materials (Protocol 10-14)

sections.title: PPE Requirements — SPRN Transport Personnel
content: MINIMUM PPE for all SPRN transport crew in patient care area:
— PAPR (powered air-purifying respirator) with hood: preferred; provides continuous positive pressure filtered air
— OR Full-face air-purifying respirator with appropriate P100/HEPA filter (if PAPR unavailable)
— Fluid-resistant coveralls (Tyvek or equivalent) covering entire body
— Double nitrile gloves (inner + outer layer)
— Boot covers
— Head cover
DRIVER (clean cab): minimum surgical mask + gloves if patient compartment is accessed; full PPE if any patient contact anticipated
Chase team members: full PPE if they will enter patient care zone
PPE donning must occur BEFORE patient contact — follow standardized agency donning sequence
PPE doffing: follow trained doffing sequence with partner assist to prevent self-contamination (Protocol 10-17)

sections.title: Equipment Inspection and Maintenance
content: All SPRN vehicles and supply caches must be inspected monthly and after each SPRN transport
PAPR units: filter replacement per manufacturer schedule; battery check before each transport
MITD: inspect for tears, port integrity, and blower motor function before each transport
Expired supplies: replace per individual item expiration dates
Post-SPRN transport: all potentially contaminated supplies disposed of as Category A biological waste (Protocol 10-15)
Restock completed within 24 hours of transport — agency responsible

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: PAPR failure during transport — switch to backup full-face respirator; do not continue without respiratory protection
Supply shortage discovered before SPRN transport — do NOT accept patient until minimum required supplies are available; notify SPRN coordinator
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. PAPR is the preferred respiratory protection for SPRN transport; full-face respirator is acceptable backup. All PPE must be donned before patient contact. Biohazard waste from SPRN transports is Category A biological waste — handle per Protocol 10-15.
